data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0840 GMT - The U.S.'s threatened tariffs on chips will likely have limited direct impact on Asia, Nomura analysts say in a research note. Given the inadequate fabrication capacity in the U.S. and the concentration of advanced node production in Asia, there is limited scope for substitution, giving the region some bargaining power, the analysts say. However, the indirect impact on Asia's electronics supply chain could be a bigger risk. "Chip tariffs will likely act as a stick and accelerate Asia's investment in U.S. fabs, which could threaten their long-term growth model." With the U.S.-China tech rivalry intensifying, Asia's tech-heavy economies are expected to face more restrictions on their sales to China and stiffer competition in both advanced and legacy chips, they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0750 GMT - Bilibili's profitability is likely to improve further, according to Daiwa analyst John Choi in a research note. "With 1Q25 gross profit margin likely to reach a similar level to 4Q24, we are optimistic about its gross margin expansion trajectory, with a long-term goal to surpass 40% level," Choi says. This is likely to be driven by high-margin core businesses, such as advertising and games, along with the flywheel effect of commercialisation, a robust content ecosystem and improving efficiency powered by AI tools, the analyst says. The analyst maintains a buy rating on the stock and raises its target price to HK$230.00 from HK$225.00. Shares are 15.5% higher at HK$178.80. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0654 GMT - NetEase may need a major blockbuster title launch for its stock to outperform in 2025, Daiwa analysts John Choi and Candis Chan say in a research note. the game maker's 4Q results didn't hold any major surprises, with revenue weighed by non-game businesses as management shifted its focus to improving profitability for these businesses, they say. The company also plans to adjust its team structures based on anticipated market demand to ensure operational efficiency, according to management. Daiwa thinks a key downside risk for NetEase would be lower-than-expected new games sales. Daiwa maintains a buy call on NetEase's H-shares but lowers its target to HK$180.00 from HK$195.00. Shares are last at HK$158.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0609 GMT - Chinese equities may face a short-term pullback although the outlook remains positive in the long term, Daiwa analysts write in a note. They suggest investors take a breather after the recent rally, which was fueled by the rise of DeepSeek and China's recent symposium with business leaders to support the private sector. Sentiment-wise, Daiwa warns that stimulus measures announced at China's Two Sessions meeting in March may disappoint investors due to elevated expectations. Additionally, the completion of U.S. reviews on reciprocal tariffs and the U.S.-China phase-one trade deal by April 1 could reignite concerns over a potential trade war, they add.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0546 GMT - Alibaba's cloud business revenue is expected to accelerate due to a strong uptick in AI demand, according to HSBC Global Research analysts. They note that the core business revenue for the December quarter was a positive surprise, driven by better-than-expected customer management, commission growth and cloud expansion. As a result, the brokerage lifts its fiscal year 2026 cloud revenue growth forecast to 17%, up from the previous 12%. Analysts also highlight the potential for upside catalysts in Alibaba's AI cloud revenue, with a surge in demand observed from January to February. Approximately 60% to 70% of new customer demand is related to AI inference, they add. HSBC maintains a buy rating for Alibaba's ADR and raises the target price to $160.00 from $128.00. The ADRs last closed at $135.97. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0354 GMT - Lenovo's integration of DeepSeek into its AI assistant could boost AI PC penetration in China by providing better AI features, DBS says. The bank maintains its forecast that Lenovo's AI PC shipments could rise 126% and 154% in fiscal 2025 and 2026, respectively. DBS expects Lenovo's server business to also remain robust in 2025, with further operating margin expansion driven by economies of scale, the removal of supply constraints in chip packaging for AI servers, and DeepSeek making large-language-model development more affordable for corporates. DBS maintains a buy call on Lenovo and keep its target at HK$18.40. Shares are last 12% higher at HK$13.14. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0352 GMT - Telstra's plan to reallocate A$800 million of capital expenditure into its mobile business over next four years should help maintain the local leadership of its network, UBS analysts say. They tell clients that the investment should improve reliability, speed and efficiency, with management continuing to focus on improving return from invested capital. They also see scope for further capital-management initiatives from Australia's largest telecommunications provider. UBS lifts its target price on the stock by 2.3% to A$4.50 and keeps a buy rating. Shares flat at A$4.14. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0323 GMT - Advanced Info Service's earnings may benefit from Thailand's likely economic growth, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart says in a research report. This should lead to increases in the Thai mobile phone operator's prepaid top-ups such as for higher data consumption, the analyst says. The brokerage now forecasts the company's 2025 core profit to grow 12%, thanks to drivers including spectrum cost savings and falling interest expenses. The company's fixed broadband revenue should also outperform its mobile revenue due to growth in its fixed broadband subscribers. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to THB312.00 from THB307.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.35% lower at THB282.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0321 GMT - NetEase's fundamentals remain intact despite its lower-than-expected 4Q mobile game revenue, HSBC analysts say in a research note. NetEase's strong deferred revenue, which implies a solid performance of legacy PC games and a successful launch of "Marvel Rivals," could outweigh weakness in its mobile games and support game growth in 2025, they say. HSBC lowers its 2025 revenue forecast slightly as NetEase focuses on lifting the profitability of its nongame segments, which could weigh on topline growth in coming quarters. However, it raises its 2025-2026 profit estimate by 3% due to the game maker's expected better cost-efficiency. HSBC raises NetEase's ADR target to $120.00 from $117.00 and keeps a buy call. Shares last ended at $101.42.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - While AI and data center demand offer long-term potential for Inari Amertron, geopolitical risks and trade tensions could weigh on growth, Kenanga IB analyst Cheow Ming Liang writes in a note. The World Semiconductor Trade Statistics expects the global semiconductor market to grow 11.2% in 2025, but uncertainties remain amid rising trade tensions, which could impact the industry's short-term outlook, he notes. Cheow revises Inari's earnings estimates for FY2025 and FY2026 lower by 21% and 23%, respectively, as the company's fiscal 1H earnings fell short of expectations. Kenanga downgrades Inari's rating to market perform from outperform, while cutting the target price to MYR2.80 from MYR3.27. Shares are 3.0% lower at MYR2.57.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0159 GMT - Singtel's new FY 2025 earnings guidance looks encouraging, the OCBC Investment Research team says in note. The telecom conglomerate has raised its FY 2025 EBIT growth forecast to high teens-to-low twenties from prior forecast of low double digits, the team says. Singtel is likely a key beneficiary of Singapore's drive for technology and innovation as highlighted in recent Singapore Budget, the team says. The government will allocate S$150 million for a new "Enterprise Compute Initiative," which Singtel could tap to expand its enterprise services and cloud-computing offerings. OCBC raises stock's fair value estimate to S$4.00 from S$3.85 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 2.1% lower at S$3.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0112 GMT - Inari Amertron could face further margin compression in 2H FY2025 due to rising overhead costs and a ramp-up in new product introductions, Maybank IB analysts Anand Pathmakanthan and Arvind Jayaratnam say in a note. They highlight that Inari may struggle to achieve its high single-digit segmental growth target with its anchor radio frequency segment posting its lowest 1H revenue in four years. The analysts cut Inari's FY2025-FY2027 earnings forecast by 26%, 14%, and 8%, respectively, citing a softer outlook. Maybank lowers Inari's target price to MYR2.70 from MYR2.93 but maintains a hold rating, noting that downside risk could be limited after a 16% share price decline since December last year. Shares are 3.8% lower at MYR2.55. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0332 GMT - Kossan Rubber Industries' earnings are expected to weaken sequentially in 1Q before improving in later quarters, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. Chee cuts the glove maker's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 49% and 31%, respectively, given its 4Q 2024 profit shortfall. The glove maker could see 8.5%-10.5% weaker sales volume for its glove and clean-room segment in 1Q as most front-loading activities by U.S. customers has already occurred in 4Q of 2024, he says. The global medical glove demand-supply balance is now projected to stabilize in 2026 instead of 2025, Chee adds. Hong Leong cuts the stock's target price to MYR2.81 from MYR3.00 but keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR1.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0045 GMT - Sonic Healthcare's earnings guidance looks achievable, if not conservative, to Morgans after the pathology services company took another step toward turning a corner in the pandemic's aftermath. Sonic expects FY 2025 Ebitda of A$1.70 billion-A$1.75 billion when currency swings are stripped out, and Morgans believes that strong underlying revenue growth supports that outlook. "After years of trying to right-size the cost base to better reflect the post Covid-19 world, labor costs are 'just about there', with operating leverage returning and profitability improving," analyst Derek Jellinek says. The bank is also upbeat about the potential for acquisitions and contract gains through the medium term. Sonic is down 1.4%. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1253 GMT - Siemens's reduced stake in Siemens Healthineers is positive for the medical-technology maker as it increases the liquidity of the stock, Equita Research analyst Davide Marchesin says in a note. The German technology company's stake sell in Siemens Healthineers isn't a surprise, aligning with previous intentions to scale down its exposure, the analyst says. Siemens is expected to provide clarity on its long-term target stake by the end of 2025, Equita Research says. Siemens shares rise 1.6% to 223.85 euros, while Siemens Healthineers shares are down 2.45% at 54.90 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

1232 GMT - Royal Philips's business is on a better footing and can match sector growth as issues related to the recall of its Respironics sleep apnea machines are fairly resolved, UBS analysts say in a note. A major product recall, persistent earnings misses and high-profile litigations clouded Philips's investment case since mid-2021 but all of these issues are now either largely resolved or priced in, the analysts say. The Dutch health-technology company is now well-placed to deliver slightly faster earnings growth over the midterm compared with the sector, with a potential portfolio change and a reentry to the sleep and respiratory market in the U.S., they say. UBS upgrades its rating on the stock to buy from neutral. Philips shares are up 0.6% at 24.23 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

1200 GMT - Royal Philips's stock is undervalued due to concerns about past problems, neglecting significant improvements in operations and earnings, UBS analysts say in a note. The Dutch health-technology company's management has made a significant turnaround in recent years, completing its sleeping device recall and major personal injury litigation. It also improved profitability and reduced debt, UBS says. UBS forecasts Philips's revenue to grow at the industry average of around 5% annually from 2025-29, with EPS increasing by an average of 10% a year. Philips's share fall of 11% alongside its 2024 results is unjustifiable, making it one of the cheapest stocks in European medtech, the analysts say. As a result, UBS changes its rating of the stock to buy from neutral. Philips shares are up 0.6% at 24.23 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0812 GMT - Standard Chartered's investors should welcome the consistency of the bank's delivery on its earnings and capital returns, Jefferies says in a research note. The lender posted fourth-quarter pre-provision earnings that beat market views, with revenue growth in its wealth solutions and financial markets divisions standing out. It also announced a $1.5 billion buyback program, surpassing expectations. The unchanged guidance for 5% to 7% revenue growth in 2025 looks conservative given the fourth-quarter's net interest income growth rate and the strong start of the year for non-interest-income items, analysts write. Shares in London open 3.7% higher at 1,182 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0619 GMT - Indian non-banking finance companies' credit costs seem to have peaked, setting the stage for improved profitability in the coming quarters, Nomura analysts say, noting the companies' 3Q FY 2025 earnings performance. With valuations having moderated, risk-reward dynamic for the sector appears more favorable now, the analysts say. A combination of stable asset quality, easing margin pressures, and better liquidity conditions could drive a re-rating in select names going forward, the analysts add. With easing liquidity conditions and more accommodative requlatory stance in India, these companies' loan disbursement growth trends should also improve in coming quarters. Nomura's top sector picks are Shriram Finance, Bajaj Finance, and Aadhar Housing Finance. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0358 GMT - The lack of a share buyback from QBE surprises its bulls at UBS. The investment bank's analysts tell clients that the Australian insurer's above-target capital ratios at the end of 2024 could have supported capital-management initiatives. The capital ratio even looks set to move higher, the UBS analysts write in a note. They reckon that QBE's annual result looked strong, with 3% gross written premium growth among the highlights. UBS has a last-published buy rating and A$23.85 target price on the stock, which is up 4.2% at A$20.92. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0340 GMT - Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing is likely to remain supported by the rise of AI, which has fueled investor sentiment towards tech stocks, supporting a surge in turnover volume, according to Citi Research analyst Michael Zhang in a note. This support should persist in the near term, and HKEX is a key beneficiary of the recovery in market sentiment toward Chinese stocks, Zhang says. The increasing number of dual-listings of mainland companies in Hong Kong could also support the recovery of the Hong Kong IPO market, Zhang adds. Citi upgrades its rating on HKEX to buy from sell, raising the target price to HK$370 from HK$275. Shares are up 5.4% at HK$345.20. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0217 GMT - CIMB Group's Indonesia unit could see an improved net interest margin in 1Q thanks to deposit repricing benefits from Bank Indonesia's January rate cut, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is expected to remain steady but unlikely to accelerate meaningfully, he reckons. With strong buffers in place, asset-quality risks appear manageable, he adds. CIMB Niaga generally contributes about 20%-25% to CIMB Group's profit before tax, he notes. CIMB's position as a large-cap, index-heavy stock makes it attractive as investors may seek refuge from uncertainties around Trump 2.0, he says. Hong Leong maintains a buy rating on CIMB and keeps the target price at MYR9.20. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR8.33. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2230 GMT - Live Nation Entertainment's plan to add 20 large venues, two of which will be located in Bogotá and Toronto, through 2026 should help the company add revenue growth. While the world's largest concert promoter is currently making money attractive returns from stadiums shows, it's not counting the beer money and the parking money it would get with its own venues. That would be a key component for Live Nation's financials given the high margins that come from the sale of beer and parking tickets. Live Nation shares fall 1.4% to $150.17 after hours. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

2211 GMT - Live Nation Entertainment is seeing consumers buy more tickets for stadium shows than ever before, CEO Michael Rapino says on a call with analysts. The world's largest concert promoter is selling over 75% of stadium tickets one week after putting them up for sale, at a much higher rate than a year earlier. There isn't a slowdown in growth at all, the executive says. "[We have] lots of lots of inventory, but equally great demand selling most of these stadiums out or are close to being sold out by the time we get to the show dates," Rapino says. Overall, Live Nation is growing its business through higher stadium volume. Shares trade 1.7% lower at $149.70 in post-market trading. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

2018 GMT - The march toward $3,000 continues for gold, with the front-month contract settling up 0.7% to a record $2,940 per troy ounce. Global economic uncertainty continues to provide upside support for gold. While the inflation picture has improved worldwide, it remains an issue, and President Trump's tariff threats add volatility, although analysts hope they are still only a leverage tool. "We await further gyrations in the Administration's tariff policies, but still believe that, at least in part, Trump will use tariffs as a negotiating tool to motivate countries to cooperate with his key priorities," Citi says in a note. SPDR Gold gains 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1845 GMT - Walmart's warning of slower sales gains, together with slowdown comparable sales growth during the holiday quarter, is sparking concerns on Wall Street. The retail giant's 4.6% growth in U.S. comparable sales in the fourth quarter from a 5.3% increase in the third quarter shows lower prices haven't been enough to drive sentiment higher among Americans. "We're growing profit faster than sales," CEO Douglas McMillon says on a call with analysts, noting that Walmart has runway to scale our higher-margin businesses like membership, marketplace and advertising. Its wholesale business, Sam's Club, also saw a sequential deceleration in comparable sales in the period, driving Costco Wholesale shares 2.4% lower. Shares of Amazon, Target and Kroger are down between 1.5% and 2%, while Walmart drops 6.5%. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1445 GMT - U.S. home values rose $2.5 trillion in 2024, reaching $49.7 trillion, a 5.2% increase--the slowest since 2019--according to Redfin. More homes are for sale, creating buyer's markets in many areas, yet prices continue to climb monthly. Redfin predicts steady price growth in 2025 due to buyer competition over limited listings compared to pre-pandemic levels. Over the past decade, U.S. home values have more than doubled from $23 trillion in 2014, peaking at $50.4 trillion in July 2024. The slight drop reflects seasonal trends, with values rising March to September and dipping in winter. New construction is also boosting market valuation. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1319 GMT - Italian lender Banco BPM should get regulatory approval to use the so-called Danish compromise in its acquisition of asset manager Anima Holding, Deutsche Bank says in a research note. The European directive allows banks to reduce the hit to their capital when purchasing asset management businesses using their insurance subsidiaries. BPM raised its offer for Anima to 7 euros per share and said it is willing to go ahead in any case, prompting UniCredit, its larger peer who launched its own takeover offer, to threaten to pull its bid. "Approval of the Danish Compromise is a matter of "when" not "if", in our view--and we believe its benefits should be reflected in the terms of Unicredit's offer," analyst Giovanni Razzoli writes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1150 GMT - Commerzbank markets a dual-tranche issue of euro-denominated benchmark three- and 10-year covered bonds Thursday. The three-year bond is a public sector covered bond, or oeffentlicher Pfandbrief, maturing in February 2028. The spread is set at midswaps plus 22 basis points, versus initial price talk of around midswaps plus 28bps, a lead manager involved in the deal says. The 10-year bond is a mortgage covered bond, or Hypothekenpfandbrief, maturing in February 2035. The spread is set at midswaps plus 46bps, versus initial price talk in the area of midswaps plus 53bps. Demand so far for each bond is more than 2.3 billion euros, the lead manager says. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0831 GMT - Sembcorp Industries is poised to post earnings CAGR of over 10% through 2028, as its three key segments enter expansion mode, DBS Group Research's Pei Hwa Ho says in a research report. Its renewables segment may deliver profit CAGR of over 20%, supported by its project pipeline which is set to increase attributable installed capacity by around 50% over next 2-3 years, the analyst says. Its gas-related services segment's earnings could increase, partly thanks to Singapore's healthy power market, the analyst says. Its integrated urban solutions segment's profit may alsoo rise, driven by potentially rapid growth in demand for industrial parks in Asean. DBS raises the stock's target price to S$7.38 from S$7.35 and maintains a buy rating. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0142 GMT - Petronas Dagangan may face risks to long-term demand from a potential expansion of Malaysia's RON95 fuel subsidy reforms, as well as from decarbonization efforts and the electric-vehicle transition trend, TA Securities analyst Hafriz Hezry says in a note. The impending subsidy cut on its own is expected to affect only the top 15% of earners in Malaysia, with likely minimal immediate impact on the Malaysian downstream petroleum product retailer's sales volume as these consumers are unlikely to significantly reduce consumption, he says. TA Securities downgrades Petronas Dagangan to sell from hold following share-price gains over the past three months. It maintains a MYR18.20 target price. Shares are 5.5% higher at MYR20.32. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1920 GMT - Crude futures hold onto moderate gains as near-term supply concerns are partly offset by a bigger-than-expected 4.6 million barrel increase in U.S. crude stockpiles despite a drop in net imports. "April WTI crude oil is seemingly willing to discount the big build, the EIA premium to the API, and the Line 16 adjustment number and instead concentrate on those rather impressive import/export dynamics," Mizuho's Robert Yawger say in a note.While imports fell by 488,000 barrels a day last week, exports grew by 472,000 b/d. March WTI goes off the board at $72.57 a barrel, up 0.4%, and the April contract rises 0.5% to $72.48. Brent gains 0.6% to $76.48 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1810 GMT - Innergex Renewable had a standout quarter, ending the year on a strong note thanks to the wind segment. Scotiabank's Robert Hope says in a research report that Ebitda proportionate came to C$210 million, topping consensus estimates of C$180 million. "The Wind segment was the key driver of the beat, though it appears that the majority of the beat was driven by one-time items," Hope says. For the year ahead, management expectations includes Ebitda proportionate of C$825 million-C$875 million and distributable cash flow per share of C$0.75-C$0.95. "Our EBITDA estimate of C$868 million is towards the top end of the range, while our distributable cash flow estimate of C$0.80 is in the bottom half of the range," the analyst says. Shares are up 8.6% to C$8.36.(adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1654 GMT - Mexico's reliance on U.S. piped gas will continue to grow, Fitch Ratings says, even as uncertainties over bilateral trade relations increase. U.S. gas imports provide reliable and cost-effective energy for Mexico, buoyed by rising demand, constrained domestic production, and pipeline infrastructure expansion, Fitch says. The market share of U.S. piped gas in Mexico is expected to increase in the coming years, according to Fitch. Trade uncertainties have emerged since President Trump came into office, but the administration has mainly focused on Mexico's exports to the U.S., and Fitch says it sees policy-driven disruptions to the gas trade as a smaller risk. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1334 GMT - Reduced oil supply from Kazakhstan and production shut-ins due to cold weather in North Dakota support oil futures, which are higher in early trade. "Add in OPEC+ members stating further delays are likely in its April production increase and the crude price positives near term seem to be outweighing the negatives," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler says in a note. Verbal conflicts between President Trump and Ukrainian President Zelensky could delay a Russian peace deal, he adds. WTI is up 0.7% at $72.74 a barrel ahead of today's March expiration. Brent is up 0.7% at $76.60 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1047 GMT - Repsol's outlook for 2025 is supportive for shares, and so too is the company's buyback plan, UBS analysts say in research note. The Spanish energy company's plan for a minimum 700 million euro buyback is 13% higher than consensus expectations of 618 million euros, the analyst say. The company is also guiding for fewer capital expenditures than were expected, which should also support Repsol's shares, they say. Operational cash flow of 6.0 billion to 6.5 billion euros, however, missed UBS forecasts, but the guidance exceeds consensus, the analysts say. Shares trade up 5.2% at 12.81 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

1042 GMT - Centrica posted lower profit and revenue for the year, as energy markets normalized and one-off recovery of costs in its British Gas division didn't repeat, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould says in a market comment. But the energy company's prior year was always going to be a tough act to follow, he says. "However, it's all about expectations for the market and Centrica came in ahead of what had been penciled in, which, along with retaining its 2025 forecast, was sufficient to get investors energized," he says. Shares trade 7.8% higher at 146.45 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

1031 GMT - Naturgy is using its balance sheet to help fix its reference shareholders' issues, analysts at RBC Capital Markets say in a research note. The Spanish natural-gas and electricity utility said it is launching a tender offer for up to 10% of its capital with the aim of increasing its free float. It guides for net debt of 16 billion euros in 2027, up from 12.2 billion euros at the end of 2024. Naturgy was the subject of a takeover attempt last year by Abu Dhabi's Taqa that failed after the suitor didn't reach an agreement with CriteriaCaixa, Naturgy's biggest shareholder. Taqa had sought to cooperate with CriteriaCaixa and two other big Naturgy shareholders--CVC Capital Partners and Global Infrastructure Partners, which is now part of BlackRock--on a possible takeover. Shares rise 4.6%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0944 GMT - Centrica posted an overall positive update, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. The U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, said its was extending its share buyback and provided clearer information on capital allocation. Earnings per share and net cash came in above consensus estimates and the dividend payout was in line, the analysts say. Centrica also gave a detailed plan on how to reduce net debt, the analysts say. Shares trade 8% higher at 146.70 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0913 GMT - Sibanye Stillwater's 2024 results were broadly in line with expectations but its 2025 guidance was weaker than expected, RBC Capital Markets analysts Marina Calero and Laura Chan write. The weaker guidance is largely driven by higher cost and capital expenditure at Stillwater and its South African platinum group metals operations, they write. Sibanye expects capital expenditure at its South African PGM operations to be around $360 million, which is 16% higher than the analysts had expected. Sibanye's all-in sustaining cost guidance of between $1,420 and $1,460 an ounce at its Stillwater facility is 23% ahead of the analysts' expectations, they add. Shares trade down 8.4% at 1,565.00 rand. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0800 GMT - Air Liquide's expectation of further operating-margin growth in the next two years should be taken positively by investors, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The French industrial-gases company introduced a new 2025-26 EBIT margin growth target of 1 percentage point, suggesting an improvement in run rate into 4Q, the analysts say. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0115 GMT - Chalice Mining's metallurgical breakthrough signals lower operating costs at its Gonneville deposit in Western Australia and reduced complexity, but it may not satisfy investors, suggests Barrenjoey. Chalice says it's now able to produce copper and nickel concentrates from low-grade composites, removing the need for a high-cost hydrometallurgical process. Analyst Richard Knights says Gonneville could now generate a 19% internal rate of return. "However, our project net present value is still comfortably less than Chalice's market cap (A$565 million), even after modelling a debt funded doubling of capacity after four years of operation." Chalice needs a step-change in palladium prices or an ability to feed higher grade material to the plant for longer to excite investors, Barrenjoey adds. It retains an underweight call on the stock. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1801 GMT - Ferrexpo says it has "not received any formal notification" that the Ukrainian government has initiated proceedings to nationalize its Poltava mining and processing plant. The London-based miner adds it is working with its legal advisors to understand the situation. The statement comes after media reports that the Ukrainian State Bureau of Investigations is seeking court approval to nationalize the mine and its assets, which are a subsidiary of Ferrexpo, because of the alleged misappropriation of around $8.86 million related to illegal mining. Shares fall 30%, to 69 pence. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1506 GMT - Shares in London-listed miner Ferrexpo plunged in mid-afternoon trade on reports that the Ukrainian government has initiated proceedings to nationalize its Poltava mining and processing plant. The mine and its assets are a subsidiary of Ferrexpo. The Ukrainian State Bureau of Investigations is seeking court approval to nationalize the assets, citing the alleged misappropriation of around $8.86 million related to illegal mining, Reuters reported earlier today. Shares fell 28% to 72.10 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1032 GMT - Anglo American's hopes of finding a buyer for its De Beers diamond unit in the near term are slim, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould writes. This is because the outlook for De Beers is so bleak as lab-grown diamonds have flooded the market, he writes. Despite the $2.9 billion write down, investors have focused on the rapid progress the London-listed miner has made since rebuffing a bid from BHP last year, Mould writes. Investors like the streamlined business but it could make it more vulnerable to further bid interest, he adds. Shares trade up 3% at 2,442.00 pence and are up 38% over the past year.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1018 GMT - Rio Tinto's earnings generation proves its diversified model works, Jefferies analysts write. The London and Sydney listed miner reported a 2% fall in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization despite an 11% decrease in benchmark iron ore prices, they write. While other miners turn their attention towards copper, Rio is positioned to continue generating strong cash flow from iron ore despite its weak price, they write. Cash flow from base iron ore will enable Rio to continue to pay a healthy dividend even in a weaker macro environment, and should make the miner relatively defensive versus its peers, they write. The analysts like Rio's defensive position and believe its shares are undervalued. Rio Tinto's London listed shares rose 0.75% to 5,074.00 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0941 GMT - Rio Tinto's 2024 results were broadly in line with expectations and the Anglo-Australian mining company provided a steady outlook for the future, energy and materials analyst at Quilter Cheviot Maurizio Carulli writes. It is reassuring to see that group earnings decreased by only 2% despite an 8% fall in iron-ore prices, he writes. While acquisitions pushed net debt up to $5.5 billion in 2024, it remains at comfortable level, he says. The miner boasts a solid portfolio of assets and the significant barriers to entry in iron ore and copper strengthen Rio Tinto's market position, he writes. Rio Tinto's London listed shares rise 0.75% to 5,074.00 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0548 GMT - China's electric-vehicle sector could see consolidation accelerating as more autonomous driving features are adopted in mass market cars, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. BYD's aggressive autonomous driving push makes higher level autonomy functions more accessible for its mass market models with price tags below CNY100,000, they add. Other Chinese automakers are likely to follow BYD's autonomous driving adoption, they say. The availability of higher-level autonomy in mass market and budget models could raise the bar in EV competition, and boost consolidation in the industry, they add. The companies with stronger AD features and higher in-house software capabilities are likely to better expand its market share, HSBC says.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0444 GMT - Grab is well positioned for growth supported by a strong balance sheet, says Deutsche Bank research team. Grab's 4Q earnings showed continued user growth, and a new high in adjusted Ebitda, making the market's reaction to the result surprising as the stock fell 11% in the after-market hours, the DB team says. DB keeps a buy rating and lifts Grab's ADR target to $7.00 from $6.00, to factor in a possible merger with Indonesian rival GoTo. There's a lot of rationale for a merger with GoTo as the combined company will likely earn 40%-50% more, they add. A merger would also lead to deeper penetration of Indonesia, they add. Grab shares last closed at $4.79. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0024 GMT - Air New Zealand's 1H result was better than expected by Forsyth Barr and was accompanied by a NZ$100 million share buyback, but still came with enough baggage to keep the bank at neutral for now. Air New Zealand signaled its engine maintenance challenges have worsened. It now expects to have up to 11 planes at any time grounded for maintenance in 2H, up from eight in 1H. "Air New Zealand is at least two years away from a normalized engine maintenance schedule, which leaves investors in limbo as to what is an appropriate and sustainable (as is possible for an airline) level of profitability to use for relative valuation means," analyst Andy Bowley says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2204 GMT - A U.S. bankruptcy court approved Spirit Airlines' restructuring plan, which will wipe out shareholders and turn the budget airline to its bondholders. Spirit had rebuffed acquisition offers from rival budget carrier Frontier Airlines in favor of pursuing its standalone plan. Spirit said it expects to emerge from chapter 11 in the coming weeks. As part of the agreement, bondholders will swap nearly $800 million of their holdings into equity into the reorganized airline and Spirit will receive $350 million of new equity investment. "We will emerge as a stronger airline with the financial flexibility to continue providing Guests with enhanced travel experiences and greater value," CEO Ted Christie said. (alison.sider@wsj.com)

1330 GMT - Mercedes-Benz's passenger-cars guidance for 2025 is broadly in line with expectations, Bernstein analysts write in a research note. The German luxury-car maker anticipates an adjusted return on sales for its passenger cars division from 6% to 8%. This matches consensus estimates of 7.1%, Bernstein says. For its vans business, the group forecasts an adjusted return on sales between 10% and 12%, below consensus of 13.3%, they say. The group's passenger cars outlook was predictably weak, but the vans guidance was softer than anticipated, the analysts say. Shares are down 1.7% at 60.13 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1105 GMT - Mercedes-Benz Group's fourth-quarter results and outlook are better than feared by some investors, Morgan Stanley's Javier Martinez de Olcoz Cerdan and Shaqeal A Kirunda say in a research note. The German luxury-car maker's quarterly earnings topped expectations, driven by a higher proportion of top-end sales that helped profitability, the analysts say. Its guidance for the year ahead is relatively in line with consensus estimates, Morgan Stanley says. However, investors' focus is on the company's plans to restore profitability, the bank says. "The sector faces cyclical, structural, and geopolitical headwinds that could last several years. [Mercedes] is not immune to those, and consensus has just partially reflected these, we think," the analysts say. Shares fall 1.9%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1020 GMT - Renault's performance for 2024 is reassuring given how most other European original equipment makers issued warnings last year, UBS analysts David Lesne and Patrick Hummel say in a research note. The French carmaker's second-half EBIT for its automotive division came in line with consensus expectations due to stronger volume growth and lower negative currency effects, the analysts say. Despite the result, UBS says it doesn't expect consensus expectations for 2025 to change much due to the lack of visibility on the effect of Europe's emissions regulation. Should the rules be delayed, or removed, consensus could rise by around 10%, UBS says. Renault shares trade 0.08% higher at 51.24 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0923 GMT - Mercedes-Benz reported solid free cash flow, but group full-year guidance is below expectations, Stifel analyst Daniel Schwarz writes. "Against...the lack of positive surprises in the results as well as a weaker outlook, we would expect a slightly negative reaction initially." However, more important will be news out of the capital markets day later today, he says. Mercedes is guiding for group revenue slightly below prior-year level, group EBIT and free cash flow significantly below prior-year level. For EBIT, "significantly" means a more than 15% decline, which implies EBIT below 11.6 billion euros. Consensus assumes 12.2 billion euros group EBIT in 2025, Schwarz says. Shares fall 2.6% to 59.59 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Bulgaria's Elhim Iskra pares loss in Jan, sales drop 25% y/y
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), February 21 (SeeNews) - Bulgarian car battery manufacturer Elhim Iskra [BUL:ELHM] said on Friday that it posted a preliminary gross loss of 198,000 levs ($105,721/101,235 euro) in January.

In the same month of 2024, Elhim Iskra booked a preliminary loss of 387,000 levs.

Sales revenue amounted to 2.64 million levs last month, Elhim Iskra said in a bourse filing. In comparison, the company recorded sales of some 3.52 million levs a year earlier.

Looking ahead, the Pazardzhik-based company, which is part of local diversified group Stara Planina Hold [BUL:SPH], projects sales of 2.74 million levs in February, down from 3.43 million levs in the same month last year.

As of 1015 CET on Friday, shares in Elhim Iskra traded 31.15% higher at 0.80 levs on the Bulgarian Stock Exchange.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: Italy Inflation Rate Confirmed at 15-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Italy rose to 1.5% in January 2025, up from 1.3% in the previous two months, in line with preliminary estimates.

This marked the sharpest increase in consumer prices since October 2023, largely driven by a sharp rise in regulated energy costs (27.5% vs. 12.7% in December), following higher power prices across select European countries due to the halt in Russian gas flows to Europe, which triggered a surge in LNG prices.

At the same time, the decline in non-regulated energy prices slowed (-3% vs. -4.2%).

Prices for recreational, cultural, and personal care services rose at a faster pace (3.3% vs. 3.1%), though this was partly offset by a slower increase in transport-related service costs (2.5% vs. 3.6%).

Meanwhile, annual core inflation remained stable at 1.8% in January.

On a monthly basis, the CPI jumped 0.6%, marking the largest increase since October 2022, accelerating from a 0.1% rise in December.

data.title: Eurozone Private Sector Activity Remains Stable in February
data.description:
The euro area's private sector maintained marginal growth in February amid falling new order levels as demand remains muted, Hamburg Commercial Bank and S&P Global's flash data showed Friday.

The HCOB Eurozone Composite PMI Output Index stood at 50.2 in February, unchanged from January and lower than the consensus estimate of 50.5.

Meanwhile, the HCOB Eurozone Services PMI Business Activity Index hit a three-month low of 50.7, down from the previous month's 51.3 and the expected 51.5.

On the manufacturing side, the PMI was up to a nine-month high of 47.3 from 46.6, against the market forecast of 47.

data.title: Sibanye Stillwater's 2025 Capex, Cost Guidance Disappoints — Market Talk
data.description: Sibanye Stillwater's 2024 results were broadly in line with expectations but its 2025 guidance was weaker than expected, RBC Capital Markets analysts Marina Calero and Laura Chan write. The weaker guidance is largely driven by higher cost and capital expenditure at Stillwater and its South African platinum group metals operations, they write. Sibanye expects capital expenditure at its South African PGM operations to be around $360 million, which is 16% higher than the analysts had expected. Sibanye's all-in sustaining cost guidance of between $1,420 and $1,460 an ounce at its Stillwater facility is 23% ahead of the analysts' expectations, they add. Shares trade down 8.4% at 1,565.00 rand. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Eurozone Ekes Out Growth as Germany Regains Footing Ahead of Election, Business Surveys Say
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

The eurozone economy continued to flatline in February, torn between signs of revival in Germany and sharp decline in France, according to business surveys released Friday.

The euro area's composite purchasing managers' index--which gauges activity among manufacturing and services companies in Europe's largest economy--held at 50.2, the same as in January, a survey compiled by Hamburg Commercial Bank and S&P Global said Friday. That was a little weaker than economists' expectations of 50.5, from a poll by The Wall Street Journal.

Coming in just above the 50 no-change mark, the survey indicates activity expanded this month, if only slightly.

Germany, Europe's largest economy, had its second straight year of contraction in 2024, but is showing signs of recovery in the early part of this year, ahead of a national election on Sunday. Investor confidence ticked higher this month in the hope that the new government might be more capable of policy action to solve the country's economic slowdown, after the incumbent coalition fell apart over disagreements on fiscal policy.

But in Germany's neighbor, France, activity sank to a 17-month low, according to the PMI data. The setback of the economy comes as a potential surprise given the recent easing of political uncertainty after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou passed a 2025 budget.

"There is certainly hope for a German government that will be able to act after the elections, which should also provide a positive impetus for the eurozone as a whole," Cyrus de la Rubia, chief economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank, said.

However, that is offset by instability in France, and a U.S. trade policy that is spreading uncertainty, he added.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Soar on Strong Tech Earnings; Alibaba Surges 15%
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks soared nearly 4% to close the week on a high note on the back of a substantial boost in Alibaba Group's profit, amid other strong tech earnings.

The Hang Seng Index surged 3.99%, or 900.94 points, to end Friday's session at 23,477.92. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index added 4.14%, or 344.16 points, to 8,666.72.

Tech stocks led the rally as a buying frenzy ensued following the strong December 2024 quarter results of Alibaba Group, China's biggest online retailer.

Its earnings are seen as a bellwether of consumer spending in the world's most populous country.

Shares of Alibaba soared nearly 15% after the e-commerce giant said it logged a 239% year-over-year rise in quarterly attributable net income to 48.9 billion yuan, beating market estimates.

Meanwhile, NetEase rose over 1% after its attributable net income for the quarter ballooned to 8.77 billion yuan from 6.58 billion yuan.

Additionally, Bilibili posted an attributable profit of 90 million yuan in Q4, rebounding from an attributable loss of 1.3 billion yuan a year prior.

Shares of the video-streaming platform closed nearly 17% higher on Friday.

data.title: Adnoc Sells $2.84 Billion of Shares in Gas Subsidiary
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. said it sold $2.84 billion of shares in Adnoc Gas, a move that will increase the subsidiary's free float and pave the way toward inclusion in share indexes.

The state-owned oil company said Friday that it sold approximately 3.1 billion ordinary shares to investors in an offering that increased the company's free float to 9% from about 5%. The settlement is expected to occur on or around Feb. 26.

The shares were priced at 3.40 dirhams per share, a 5% discount to the closing share price of 3.58 dirhams on Feb. 20, which was the last trading day before the sale.

The sale represented 4% of Adnoc Gas' total share capital, the company said.

The company said the higher free float is expected to provide a pathway toward inclusion in the MSCI Emerging Markets Index and the FTSE Emerging Market Index. That could take place at the next quarterly review and is subject to Adnoc Gas meeting all the relevant inclusion criteria, Adnoc said.

Adnoc Group will continue to retain a majority 86% shareholding in the gas business and said it will not sell further shares for a period of six months.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Magellanic Cloud Issues Letter of Intent to Acquire Indian Financial Technology Company
data.description:
Magellanic Cloud has issued a letter of intent to acquire of an undisclosed India-based financial technology company at an estimated purchase price of 320 million Indian rupees, according to a Friday filing to the BSE.

The acquisition is subject to the completion of the ongoing due diligence process.

The company aims to finalize the share purchase agreement by March 15, subject to the acceptance of the terms of the letter of intent.

Shares of the company were up nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Euro Area Service Activity Eases in February
data.description:
The HCOB Eurozone Services PMI fell to 50.7 in February 2025, the lowest in three months, down from 51.3 in January and missing market expectations of 51.5.

While services activity expanded for the third consecutive month, growth was modest and the weakest in this sequence.

New business declined for the first time in three months, though employment saw a slight increase.

Input cost inflation remained elevated at the same rapid pace as January, while output prices continued to rise solidly.

Meanwhile, business sentiment weakened.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Friday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Oil prices end higher as investors shake off rise in U.S. crude inventories

Oil futures ended higher Thursday, with investors shaking off a larger-than-expected rise in U.S. crude inventories.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by more than expected last week in a fourth consecutive build, while product stocks declined, according to U.S. government data.

U.S. Natural Gas Snaps 7-Session Winning Streak

U.S. natural gas futures snapped a seven-session winning streak as near-term forecasts point to milder weather to follow this week's Arctic blast.

How Tariffs Could Shock America's Power System

Transformers used in power grids are especially vulnerable to trade disruptions.

Schneider Electric Beats Market Views

The energy-management and automation group posted higher net profit and revenue for 2024 on continued strength in energy management, beating market views.

Activist Elliott to Seek Phillips 66 Board Seats

With a more-than-$2.5 billion stake in Phillips 66, Elliott has pushed for a streamlining of the oil refiner and seeks more representation on the company's board.

Repsol Swings to Loss on Production, Energy Price Declines

Repsol reported a decline in earnings, swinging to a quarterly loss, after production and energy prices fell in the final quarter of last year

Occidental Stock Drops After Mixed Results. What's Dragging on Buffett's Favorite.

OXY stock has lost 19% over the past year. Weak crude prices are to blame.

Diamondback Energy to Acquire Double Eagle

The oil and natural gas company reached an agreement with Double Eagle in exchange for 6.9 million shares of its common stock and $3 billion in cash.

Galp Misses Expectations; Launches Buyback

The oil-and-gas producer launched a $262 million share buyback and said its results reflected a strong performance despite a volatile economic environment.

3.        OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer: Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state “none”
  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to: (i)        the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or (ii)        the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced: If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state “none”
  • None
Date of disclosure:21st February 2025

Telephone number:+91 9768034903

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

The Panel’s Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code’s dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel’s website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

data.title: E.ON: Consortium Led By Co Gets 45-Mln-Euro EU Funding To Expand EV Charging Network
data.description:
E ON SE :

  • CONSORTIUM LED BY E.ON RECEIVES 45 MILLION EUROS IN EU FUNDING TO EXPAND CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE FOR ELECTRIC CARS AND COMMERCIAL VEHICLES IN EUROPE

  • DRIVE-E GLOBAL PROJECT IS TO BE COMPLETED BY 2027 AND WILL STRENGTHEN THE CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE ALONG IMPORTANT TRAFFIC ROUTES
